2605.23296.title: Parallel Context Compaction for Long-Horizon LLM Agent Serving
2605.23296.paper_summary: Long-horizon LLM agents accumulate growing conversation histories that eventually exceed the model's context window. Context compaction via LLM-based summarization keeps the conversation bounded, but summarization is inherently lossy and the blocking call stalls agent inference for tens of seconds. Moreover, the operator has no fine-grained control over summary volume since prompt instructions are largely ignored, and as context grows, both the amount of output tokens the model produces and the information it retains fluctuate substantially from run to run, making the agent's retained knowledge unpredictable across runs. We introduce \textbf{parallel compaction} for long-horizon agentic flows and characterize it against the sequential synchronous baseline across four backbones spanning 8B to 120B parameters, mixing dense and MoE architectures with reasoning and non-reasoning models, on the HotpotQA multi-hop QA and LoCoMo long-context dialogue benchmarks. Parallel compaction gives the operator fine-grained, predictable control over summary volume and enables more targeted prompt engineering per block. At matched compaction decode volume, it reduces end-to-end wall time and improves compaction throughput over the sequential baseline.
2605.23296.paper_summary_zh: 長期視野的 LLM 代理累積逐漸增長的對話歷史，最終超過模型的上下文窗口。通過 LLM 基於摘要的上下文壓縮保持對話的邊界，但摘要本質上是有損的，且阻塞調用會使代理推理停滯數十秒。此外，操作員對摘要量沒有細粒度的控制，因為提示指令在很大程度上被忽略，隨著上下文的增長，模型產生的輸出標記數量和它保留的信息在不同運行之間波動很大，使得代理保留的知識在不同運行中變得不可預測。我們引入了 \textbf{平行壓縮} 用於長期視野的代理流，並在四個涵蓋 8B 到 120B 參數的骨幹上，對其進行了與順序同步基準的特徵描述，混合了密集和 MoE 架構，以及推理和非推理模型，針對 HotpotQA 多跳 QA 和 LoCoMo 長上下文對話基準。平行壓縮使操作員對摘要量具有細粒度、可預測的控制，並使每個區塊的提示工程更加針對性。在匹配的壓縮解碼量下，它減少了端到端的牆時並提高了壓縮吞吐量，相較於順序基準。
2605.23296.author: Musa Cim et.al.
2605.23296.authors: Musa Cim, Burak Topcu, Chita Das, Mahmut Taylan Kandemir
